narrative.reason: Q2 opens with UAL pricing 43% above the algo recommendation; the policy gap is widest here.
Fri business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
First demand shock: weather and event spillovers shifted late-booking behavior; pricing desk nonresponsive.
Delta undercuts by $15; algo identifies elastic demand window that static pricing misses entirely.
Fri business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Mid-quarter review: cumulative lift tracking $280K ahead of actual; share advantage stabilizing.
Peak share day: UAL captures 72% seat share as policy exploits Delta response lag on Monday business mix.
Thu business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Fri business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Thu business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Fri business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Mon business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Validation period begins: out-of-sample performance tracks in-sample within 3% MAPE.
Worst pricing gap: validation period shows $95/seat mispricing on Sat leisure demand.
Fri business mix: the pricing team stayed sticky while the policy adjusted into the local elasticity sweet spot.
Q2 close: $567K total counterfactual lift confirmed; Nash equilibrium reached by day 20.

weatherShocks.description: Severe thunderstorm line caused 3-hour ground stop. 142 flights cancelled, FAA ATCSCC advisory issued.
Late-season snow squall with 0.5in accumulation. De-icing delays averaged 45min. Wind gusts 38kts.
Visibility below 1/4 mile until 11am CDT. Arrival rate cut to 60% of normal. 87 diversions.
Low-level wind shear reported on approach. Multiple go-arounds on Rwy 10L/28R.
Supercell produced golf-ball hail and 60kt microburst. Airport closed 90 minutes. 200+ cancellations.
IFR conditions with 400ft ceiling persisted most of day. Reduced throughput 30%.
Embedded thunderstorms in approach corridor. Multiple holding patterns, avg delay 52min.
Heat index 105°F. Ramp operations slowed, some regional aircraft weight-restricted.
Mesoscale convective system with 75kt straight-line winds. Full ground stop, terminal evacuated.
Visibility 2-3mi in wildfire smoke haze. VFR-to-IFR transition complicated approaches.
Nor'easter brought heavy rain and 45kt gusts. Ground delay program for all arrivals.
Morning fog reduced visibility to 1/2 mile. Cat II/III approaches required until noon.
Rapid wind shift required runway configuration change. 30-min gap in operations.
Fast-moving squall line crossed NYC airspace. 2-hour ground stop, 156 flights impacted.
Temporary flight restriction over Manhattan constrained departure routes.
Holiday traffic surge combined with afternoon convection. Delays averaged 40min.
Tropical moisture plume brought persistent heavy rain. IFR conditions all day.
FAA ATC staffing at 75%. Mile-in-trail restrictions applied. GDP issued 6am-2pm.
Pavement temperature 145°F. Speed restrictions on taxiways. Minor departure delays.
